prompt_text: How do I build a beautiful careers site and track all applicants in one place without spending a lot on software?
response:
The lowest-cost way to do this is to use an **all-in-one ATS with a careers-page builder** so your site and applicant tracking live in one system, with automatic job updates and no custom development. Platforms like **Recooty**, **GoHire**, **Workable**, and **JBoard** all advertise branded careers pages plus centralized candidate tracking, with Recooty explicitly offering a free careers page option and GoHire/Workable emphasizing integrated ATS workflows.[1][2][4][5]

To get a **beautiful careers site** without spending much, look for these features:
- **Branded templates** so you can launch quickly without a designer.[1][2][4][5]
- **Auto-syncing job listings** so filled roles disappear and new roles appear automatically.[1][2]
- **Company-story sections** for your mission, values, photos, testimonials, benefits, and open roles.[1][2]
- **Custom domain or embedded widget** so the careers page matches your main website.[2][4]
- **Mobile-friendly design** and simple drag-and-drop editing.[2][4][5]

To **track all applicants in one place**, choose software with a built-in **ATS dashboard** that centralizes applications, candidate stages, notes, messaging, and interview scheduling.[1][2][4][5] GoHire says its platform includes one-click job posting, candidate tracking, interview scheduling, and reporting, while Recooty describes a central dashboard for reviewing, rating, and hiring candidates collaboratively.[1][2]

If you want to keep software costs down, the best practical options from the results are:
- **Recooty** if you want a **free or low-cost branded careers page** to start fast.[2]
- **JBoard** if you want a more configurable job-board-style site with ATS and candidate messaging.[4]
- **GoHire** if you want a straightforward all-in-one recruiting tool with careers pages and tracking.[1]
- **Workable** if you can pay more for a more mature recruiting suite and talent database.[5]

A simple low-budget setup is:
- Buy or use a domain you already own.
- Pick a platform with a **template-based careers page** and **built-in ATS**.[1][2][4][5]
- Add your logo, mission, values, benefits, photos, and testimonials.[1][2]
- Embed the careers page or subdomain on your website.[2][4]
- Post jobs once, then let the system sync applications into one candidate pipeline.[1][2][5]

If you want, I can also compare the cheapest options side by side and recommend the best fit based on your team size and budget.
